# BUG_REPORT — GLM Safety & Compatibility Review

*Generated by glm-5.1*

---

## Overall Verdict: ✅ SAFE TO IMPLEMENT, with caveats

Every fix addresses something that is already broken or insecure. The "breaking" changes are **intentional security restrictions** — they break insecure behavior on purpose. Just test the extension end-to-end after each change.

---

## ✅ Safe to fix immediately (no workflow risk — these are already broken)

| Bug | Why Safe |
|-----|----------|
| B-004 | Rate limiter is already **permanently broken** — fixing it only restores intended behavior |
| B-005/B-006 | Backups are already **corrupt** — fixing atomicity only helps |
| B-007 | ONNX blocks event loop — offloading to thread pool only improves responsiveness |
| B-008 | CTC layout produces **wrong results silently** — fixing only makes results correct |
| B-012/B-013 | Migrations already **fail on fresh DB** — fixing them only enables setup |
| B-014 | Missing async DB driver — app already **crashes on first query** |
| B-015 | Test-only bug, no prod impact |
| B-016 | Guard against submit-before-start — purely defensive |
| B-023 | JSON injection fix — purely fixes malformed logs |
| B-026 | Direct key access 500 — purely fixes a crash |
| F-001 | dashboardPage remount — **only improves performance**, no behavior change |
| F-002 | useKeyHandlers reset crash — already crashes, fixing only helps |
| F-003/F-004/F-005 | Toast timeout, JSON.parse, clipboard — all purely defensive |
| E-011 | Map memory leak — purely improves memory |
| I-001/I-002 | Workflow/deploy paths already **broken** — fixing only enables CI/CD |
| I-005 | Port mismatch already **causes connection failures** |

---

## ⚠️ Moderate risk — safe IF done carefully

| Bug | Risk | Mitigation |
|-----|------|------------|
| **B-001** (solver CancelledError) | Must ensure future resolution doesn't change existing caller expectations | Test with in-flight requests during stop() |
| **B-002/B-003** (exam/cache thread safety) | Adding locks changes timing — could expose previously-hidden race conditions in callers | Add locks incrementally; test under concurrent load |
| **B-009** (routes.py key create/revoke) | **Intentional access restriction** — existing non-master-key users will LOSE the ability to create/revoke keys | This is a security fix. Document that only master key holders can manage keys going forward |
| **B-010** (pkill -9 restart) | Replacing with graceful shutdown needs careful implementation — must ensure restart actually works | Test restart flow end-to-end after fix |
| **B-011** (asyncio.run in payments) | Changing to `await` fixes notifications — but if Telegram bot was down, users may suddenly start getting notifications | Low risk; beneficial change |
| **B-021** (subscription bypasses user block) | **Intentional behavior change** — blocked users can no longer subscribe | This is a security fix, not a regression |
| **B-022** (relative paths in config.yaml) | Changing to absolute paths could break existing deployments that rely on CWD | Use `Path(__file__).parent` resolution; add override via env var |
| **B-024** (QR endpoint no auth) | **Existing unauthenticated access will break** | This is a security fix. Add `_admin_guard` and document the change |
| **B-035** (weak session cookie) | **All existing admin sessions invalidated** on next startup | One-time re-login required. Low impact. |

---

## 🔴 High risk — needs careful staging

| Bug | Risk | Mitigation |
|-----|------|------------|
| **E-001** (postMessage `'*'`) | **BIGGEST BREAKING CHANGE** — any cross-origin message flow that relied on `'*'` will break. This affects 10+ files across the extension | Change to `window.location.origin` but **test EVERY module**: captcha, exam, autofill, sarathi_panel, vcam, userscript_engine. Each one has its own postMessage calls |
| **E-002** (new Function(code)) | Restricting arbitrary code execution could break existing userscripts that rely on it | Only restrict sender validation — let signed/known scripts continue to execute |
| **E-005** (`<all_urls>` scope) | **Extension will stop working on non-target domains** (intentional!) | This is a security fix. But verify the target domain list covers ALL your use cases |
| **E-007/E-008** (dialog/fetch override scope) | Scoping to target domain means the overrides **won't apply** on other sites | Intentional. But if any workflow relies on overrides on non-target sites, it breaks |
| **E-010** (sender validation) | Adding origin checks to `onMessage` could reject legitimate messages from content scripts | Whitelist `chrome-extension://` origin + target site origins |

---

## What WILL break (intentionally)

- Non-master-key users can no longer create/revoke API keys (B-009)
- Unauthenticated QR image access will be blocked (B-024)
- Extension will no longer run on `<all_urls>` — only target domains (E-005)
- `browsingData.remove({since:0})` will no longer wipe ALL sites (E-006)
- `alert`/`confirm` overrides will only apply on target domain (E-007)
- Fetch/XHR interceptors will only apply on target domain (E-008)
- `postMessage('*')` will be restricted to same-origin (E-001)

**These are all intentional security hardening — they break insecure behavior, not working features.**

---

## Recommended Implementation Order

1. Security fixes (S-01→S-05 from Security Audit)
2. Critical bug fixes (B-001→B-015, F-001→F-002, E-001→E-010)
3. High bug fixes (B-016→B-026, F-003→F-006, E-011→E-014)
4. Medium/Low bug fixes
5. **Test extension end-to-end after E-001 (postMessage) fix** — single biggest workflow-impacting change
